entitySuffixRule: Must contain "limited liability company" or "limited company," or the abbreviation "L.L.C.," "LLC," "L.C.," or "LC." "Limited" may be abbreviated as "Ltd." and "company" as "Co."
distinguishabilityNote: Must be distinguishable upon the records of the Secretary of State from every existing entity name and from every name that is reserved or registered.
nameReservationCostBasis: The $25 is the fee on the Application to Reserve a Limited Liability Company Name (Form F0038). The reservation is nonrenewable and holds the name for 120 days.
notes: The restricted terms come from South Carolina's separate licensing laws, not the LLC name statute: banking terms (bank, banking, trust, savings and loan) under Title 34 and insurance terms (insurance, assurance) under Title 38 require the business to be licensed for that activity before the name is accepted. South Carolina LLCs do not file an annual report with the Secretary of State, so after formation the reservation is one of the few name-related filings the office handles.

sources.title: Name; Reserved name (Uniform Limited Liability Company Act)
